title: hieracium maculatum subsp. lecokianum
intro: Hieracium maculatum subsp. lecokianum, also known as spotted hawkweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae). It is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating digestive issues, and treating respiratory problems. Hieracium maculatum subsp. lecokianum is often prepared as a tea or infusion, and its leaves and flowers can be used in herbal remedies, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
